message.content:
<deliberation>
Approach:
- Reviewed each main GDPR node against the entire provided policy chunk.
- Pulled in 1-hop cross-references where the main node depends on other Articles/paragraphs for context.
- Looked for concrete contradictions, narrowings, or other taxonomy-defined deviations. Per instruction, simple omissions are treated as Non-Deviation.

Policy excerpts used:
- Erasure exceptions: “A right to erasure does not exist insofar as • the data may not be deleted due to a legal obligation or must be processed due to a legal obligation; • the data processing is necessary for the assertion, exercise or defence of legal claims.”
- Restriction: “Right to restrict processing: You have the right to request that we restrict the processing of your personal data.”
- Portability: “Right to data portability: You have the right to receive from us the data you have provided in a structured, common and machine-readable format, as well as the right to have this data transferred to another controller. This right only exists if • you have provided us with the data on the basis of consent or on the basis of a contract concluded with you; • the processing is carried out by automated means.”
- Objection: “Right to object to processing: If the processing of your data is based on Art 6 para 1 lit f) GDPR, you may object to the processing at any time.”

Node-by-node reasoning:
1) REG-031 (Art20(1)) with refs REG-001 (Art6(1)) and REG-009 (Art9(2)):
- Policy mirrors the GDPR conditions: processing based on consent or contract and carried out by automated means, and provides for receipt and transfer to another controller. The GDPR’s “without hindrance” element is not stated, but this is an omission, not a deviation.

2) REG-035 (Art21(1)) with ref REG-001 (Art6(1)):
- Policy states objection right for Art6(1)(f). GDPR also includes Art6(1)(e) and “including profiling,” plus the “compelling legitimate grounds” and “legal claims” caveats. The policy does not contradict these; it simply omits them. No deviation identified.

3) REG-017 (Art14(2)) with refs REG-016 (Art14(1)), REG-001 (Art6(1)), REG-009 (Art9(2)), REG-041 (Art22(1)):
- Policy chunk presents some data subject rights but does not supply the full set of Art14(2) elements (retention period, legitimate interests details, consent withdrawal, complaint right, source, ADM/profiling info). These are omissions per instruction, not deviations. No conflicting statements found.

4) REG-013 (Art13(2)) with refs REG-012 (Art13(1)), REG-001 (Art6(1)), REG-009 (Art9(2)), REG-041 (Art22(1)):
- Similar to Art14(2), the policy chunk mentions certain rights but omits several required disclosures (retention period, consent withdrawal, complaint right, statutory/contractual requirement and consequences, ADM/profiling info). Omissions only; no contradictions. No deviation.

5) REG-028 (Art18(1)) with ref REG-035 (Art21(1)):
- Policy grants a general right to request restriction without listing the four GDPR grounds (accuracy contested, unlawful processing with opposition to erasure, data needed for legal claims, or pending Art21(1) objection). Absence of conditions is an omission, not an over-commitment that contradicts Art18(1). No deviation.

Conclusion:
- No explicit contradictions, narrowings, or other deviations detected in the provided chunk against the five main nodes when read with their cross-references. All gaps are omissions and must be classified as Non-Deviation under the instructions.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-031",
    "clause": "Art20(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
```
